titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Chargé in Costa Rica ( (Trueblood))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Chargé in Costa Rica ( Trueblood )
div.p: The Secretary of State encloses, at the request of the Export-Import Bank, for transmission to the Costa Rican Minister of FinanceAlvaro Bonilla Lara. a letter addressed to him on August 23, 1944, by the President of the Bank.Warren Lee Pierson; letter not printed.

The particular attention of the Embassy is directed to the last paragraph of the Bank’s letter stating that: “In view of the foregoing as well as the intention of your Government to buttress President Picado’s fiscal policies by the adoption of various reforms recommended by Messrs. Ness and Kekich,Thomas Kekich, Bureau of Accounts, Treasury Department. we believe, Mr. Minister, that you will concur in the opinion that consideration of an amendment of our Agreement of July 9, 1942This loan agreement, not printed, was drafted in accordance with terms described in Department’s telegram 264, June 27, 1942, 3 p.m., to San José, Foreign Relations, 1942, vol. vi, p. 251. should be postponed.” The Bank refers to a number of recommendations for the improvement and strengthening of the methods of handling Costa Rican finances suggested by Messrs. Ness and Kekich of the United States Treasury Department who, it will be recalled, made studies of the Costa Rican fiscal situation in 1942 and 1943 at the request of that Government.

For the Embassy’s confidential information, during the recent discussions in Washington Señor PeñaJulio Peña Morua, General Manager of the National Bank of Costa Rica. suggested that if the BankPresumably the Export-Import Bank of Washington. agreed to the proposed amendment to the 1942 agreement, it should do so only on condition that the Costa Rican Government adopt the fiscal reforms which it had said it intended to make.
